message:
fix(frankenterm): handle RecvError::Cancelled in notification subscriber pipeline

`run_watcher`'s notification pipeline reads from a subscriber with
`frankenterm_core::events::EventSubscriber::recv_with_cx(...)` and
matches on the resulting `Result<_, RecvError>`. The existing match
handled `Lagged`, `Closed`, and the happy path, but `RecvError` grew
a `Cancelled` variant when core event-bus recv became cx-aware — and
the watcher's cx can be cancelled legitimately during graceful
shutdown. Without the new arm, the match was non-exhaustive and the
compile failed under the current core surface.

Adds a `Err(RecvError::Cancelled) => { tracing::info!("Notification
subscriber cancelled"); break; }` arm so the subscriber drops out of
its recv loop, logs the reason, and allows the surrounding
`"Notification pipeline stopped"` info message to fire as it already
does on `Closed`. Matches the handling pattern used elsewhere in this
file (distributed agent stream, lifecycle watchers) and preserves
the existing lagged/closed semantics.
